title: ERROR (catatonit:2): failed to exec pid1: No such file or directory on
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

With the testing podman from `https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/testing/xUbuntu_20.04`, I'm getting:

```
2021-03-05T13:06:53.583 > sudo /home/ubuntu/cephtest/cephadm --image quay.ceph.io/ceph-ci/ceph:49c09faafe9891422be520f56474c0bc9f5ef70a shell -c /etc/ceph/ceph.conf -k /etc/ceph/ceph.client.admin.keyring --fsid 4f62a35a-7db3-11eb-9069-001a4aab830c -- ceph orch host ls --format=json
2021-03-05T13:06:56.210 stderr:ERROR (catatonit:2): failed to exec pid1: No such file or directory
2021-03-05T13:07:06.597 stderr:level=error msg="Error removing container 84e3d2b2aa49ed159b93c71a35f634fccb0aeb60f511946ea51bb0cc80b63e60: error removing container 84e3d2b2aa49ed159b93c71a35f634fccb0aeb60f511946ea51bb0cc80b63e60 root filesystem: 1 error occurred:\n\t* unlinkat /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/bb639d42bdd091a9c0ba250cba436637b6c0d582da0077c8fad8d2310875e216/merged: device or resource busy\n\n"
```

Full log is here: https://pulpito.ceph.com/swagner-2021-03-05_12:04:33-rados:cephadm-wip-swagner2-testing-2021-03-05-1040-pacific-distro-basic-smithi/5939138/

downstream bug is here: https://tracker.ceph.com/issues/49633


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Looks like this was introduced on Friday last week or so as we haven't seen this issue before

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

physical

comments.body:
Can you provide the full Podman command being executed by `cephadm`?

My initial impression is that `--init` is being used, and catatoinit is reporting that your requested command (`shell -c`) is not found in the container. Did you mean to type `sh -c`?

Yeah, we're using `podman --init`, but interestingly, this fails only with about 10%-20% probability:

https://pulpito.ceph.com/swagner-2021-03-12_11:27:39-rados:cephadm:smoke-wip-swagner-testing-2021-03-11-1320-distro-basic-smithi/

There is a thing that is even more confusing to me: The error message strangely differs. 

1. `ERROR (catatonit:2): failed to exec pid1: No such file or directory`
2. ``Error: executable file `ceph` not found in $PATH: No such file or directory: OCI not found``
3. `Error: error looking up supplemental groups for container ca2233e555ae8ce7d36d0d9febddc59e395702db9da66664ca495315f6ccc323: Unable to find group disk`

According to our statistics, we have 41 failed runs with (1), 10 failures with (2) and another ten failed runs with (3)

And I'm not yet able to make **any** sense out of it.

> Can you provide the full Podman command being executed by `cephadm`?
> 
> My initial impression is that `--init` is being used, and catatoinit is reporting that your requested command (`shell -c`) is not found in the container. Did you mean to type `sh -c`?

The full podman command looks something like
```
/bin/podman run --rm --ipc=host --net=host --privileged --group-add=disk --init -e CONTAINER_IMAGE=quay.ceph.io/ceph-ci/ceph:49c09faafe9891422be520f56474c0bc9f5ef70a -e NODE_NAME=dael -e CEPH_USE_RANDOM_NONCE=1 -v /var/run/ceph/63c0337e-c1b0-443b-9452-4c1c5a96a78b:/var/run/ceph:z -v /var/log/ceph/63c0337e-c1b0-443b-9452-4c1c5a96a78b:/var/log/ceph:z -v /var/lib/ceph/63c0337e-c1b0-443b-9452-4c1c5a96a78b/crash:/var/lib/ceph/crash:z -v /dev:/dev -v /run/udev:/run/udev -v /sys:/sys -v /var/lib/ceph/63c0337e-c1b0-443b-9452-4c1c5a96a78b/selinux:/sys/fs/selinux:ro -v /run/lvm:/run/lvm -v /run/lock/lvm:/run/lock/lvm -v /etc/ceph/ceph.conf:/etc/ceph/ceph.conf:z -v /etc/ceph/ceph.client.admin.keyring:/etc/ceph/ceph.keyring:z --entrypoint ceph quay.ceph.io/ceph-ci/ceph:49c09faafe9891422be520f56474c0bc9f5ef70a orch host ls --format=json
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@liewegas are you still seeing this issue with the latest podman version?
we're no longer seeing this in Ceph's CI.
